sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Judy Gomez, is a 62-year-old female who presents for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports that she has been doing well since her last visit, and her symptoms have improved with the current treatment regimen.
She mentions that she occasionally experiences increased pain in her feet when she has a day off from her desk job and is more active, but this is not frequent.

She denies taking methotrexate on the day of her COVID-19 vaccination, which she believes may have reduced the efficacy of the vaccine, and reports feeling unwell after the first dose but tolerating the second dose after consulting the hotline.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of psoriatic arthritis, for which she is currently being treated with methotrexate and prednisone.
She has had a recent COVID-19 vaccination, for which she did not take methotrexate on the day of the first dose.
Review of systems: The patient reports bruising, particularly on her skin, which she attributes to taking prednisone.
Current medications: I prescribed methotrexate for psoriatic arthritis.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has a good range of motion in her toes and knees, but reports pain in her joint areas, particularly in her feet and knees.
She has ridges on her nails, which are consistent with psoriasis.
Examination of the skin reveals bruising.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has psoriatic arthritis, which is being managed with methotrexate and prednisone, with plans to discontinue prednisone and continue with methotrexate.
Problem list: Psoriatic arthritis; suspected bruising due to prednisone use.
Plan:
I discontinued the patient's prednisone and instructed her to continue with methotrexate.
I also instructed her to schedule an appointment for blood work at the front desk and to return in three months.
Follow-up: The patient is to return in three months for follow-up.
